info.title: Rclone RC API
info.description:
Comprehensive documentation for the Rclone Remote Control (RC) API. Rclone RC provides an HTTP API to control rclone programmatically — manage remotes, transfer files, sync directories, mount filesystems, and monitor jobs.

## Getting Started

Start the rclone RC server:

```bash
# Start with default settings (rclone rc on localhost:5572)
rclone rcd --rc-no-auth --rc-allow-origin='https://rclone-rc-api.cloudnrain.com'

rclone rcd --rc-no-auth --rc-allow-origin='localhost:5173' # If you spin up this vite app

# Start with authentication
rclone rcd --rc-user=admin --rc-pass=secret

# Start with custom address
rclone rcd --rc-addr=:8080 --rc-no-auth

# Start with web GUI
rclone rcd --rc-web-gui --rc-no-auth
```

## Authentication

By default, rclone requires authentication for methods that access remotes. You can configure auth with:

- `--rc-user` / `--rc-pass` — Basic HTTP authentication
- `--rc-htpasswd` — htpasswd file for authentication
- `--rc-no-auth` — Disable authentication (use only in trusted environments)

## Special Parameters

All endpoints accept these special underscore-prefixed parameters:

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `_async` | boolean | Run the command asynchronously. Returns a `jobid` immediately. |
| `_config` | object | Override global config flags for the duration of this call. |
| `_filter` | object | Set file filters for the duration of this call. |
| `_group` | string | Assign a stats group name for metrics tracking. |

## Input Methods

All endpoints accept `POST` requests. Parameters can be sent as:

- **JSON body** (recommended): `Content-Type: application/json`
- **URL query parameters**: `?param=value`
- **Form data**: `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded`

JSON body takes precedence when multiple methods are used.

## Error Handling

Errors return a non-2xx HTTP status with a JSON body:

```json
{
  "error": "description of error",
  "input": { "original": "parameters" },
  "status": 500,
  "path": "endpoint/path"
}
```

tags.name: RC
tags.description: Remote control utility endpoints — echo, list commands, and testing.

tags.name: Core
tags.description: Core system operations — version info, memory stats, bandwidth, process control, transfer stats, and garbage collection.

tags.name: Config
tags.description: Manage rclone configuration — create, read, update, and delete remotes.

tags.name: Operations
tags.description: File and directory operations — list, copy, move, delete, mkdir, stat, upload, and more.

tags.name: Sync
tags.description: Synchronization operations — copy, move, sync, and bisync between remotes.

tags.name: Mount
tags.description: Mount and unmount remote filesystems locally via FUSE.

tags.name: VFS
tags.description: Virtual File System operations — refresh cache, manage upload queues, and VFS stats.

tags.name: Job
tags.description: Async job management — list, monitor status, stop, and batch execute jobs.

tags.name: Serve
tags.description: Start and stop file serving protocols — HTTP, WebDAV, FTP, SFTP, NFS, etc.

tags.name: Options
tags.description: Read and modify global rclone options at runtime.

tags.name: Cache
tags.description: Cache backend operations — expire entries, fetch chunks, and view stats.

tags.name: Debug
tags.description: Debug and profiling endpoints — memory limits, profile rates, and GC tuning.

basicAuth.description: HTTP Basic Authentication using --rc-user and --rc-pass credentials.

post.description: Echoes back all input parameters. Useful for testing connectivity.
Same as rc/noop but requires authentication.
Returns a JSON object listing all registered RC commands with their descriptions.
Returns an error response. Used for testing error handling.
Returns a fatal error. Used for testing fatal error handling.
Panics the server. **Warning**: May crash the server if panic recovery is not enabled.
Returns detailed version information including OS, architecture, and Go version.
Returns the PID of the running rclone process.
Returns Go runtime memory statistics including heap allocation and GC stats.
Triggers Go runtime garbage collection.
Returns disk usage for a local directory.
Query or set bandwidth limit. Supports separate upload/download limits with `upload:download` syntax.
Returns comprehensive transfer statistics.
Resets transfer statistics counters.
Deletes a specific stats group.
Returns list of completed transfers (up to 100).
Returns all stats group names.
Gracefully terminates the rclone process.
Obscures a plaintext password using rclone's algorithm.
Execute any rclone command via the RC API.
Creates a new remote configuration.
Updates an existing remote configuration.
Deletes a remote by name.
Returns configuration for a specific remote.
Returns full configuration for all remotes.
Returns array of configured remote names.
Sets password parameters for a remote (auto-obscured).
Returns paths to config file, cache, and temp directories.
Returns all available storage providers with config options.
Changes the rclone config file path.
Unlocks an encrypted configuration file.
Lists files and directories in a remote path. Supports recursion, hashes, metadata, and filtering.
Returns info about a single file or directory. Returns null if not found.
Returns storage space information (total, used, free, trashed).
Creates a directory at the specified path.
Removes an empty directory. Fails if not empty.
Removes empty directories under a path.
Recursively removes a directory and contents. **Destructive.**
Copies a file between remotes.
Moves a file between remotes. Source deleted after copy.

post.summary: Copy a URL to a remote
post.description: Downloads from URL and saves to remote.
Removes all files in path (not directories). **Destructive.**
Deletes a single file.
Cleans up trash/old versions (if backend supports it).
Returns features, hashes, and metadata capabilities.

post.summary: Get total size of a remote
post.description: Returns total file count and bytes.
Creates public link to file/directory.
Produces hashsum for all files in path.
Returns hash of a single file.
Checks for differences between source and destination.
Changes storage tier for all files.

post.summary: Set storage tier for a file
post.description: Changes storage tier for one file.
Uploads a file via multipart form data.
Copies files from source to destination, skipping existing. Use with `_async=true` for large transfers.
Moves files from source to destination. Source deleted after copy.
Makes destination match source, **deleting extra files on destination**. Use `_async=true`.
Bidirectional sync — changes on either side propagated to the other.
Mounts a remote filesystem to a local mount point via FUSE.
Unmounts a previously mounted filesystem.
Unmounts all currently mounted filesystems.
Returns all currently active mount points.
Returns available mount types.
Refreshes VFS directory cache.
Removes files/dirs from VFS cache.
Returns all active VFS instances.
Returns VFS stats including disk cache and metadata cache.
Gets or sets poll interval for change detection.
Returns pending uploads.
Sets expiry time for a queue item.
Returns all async jobs.
Returns current status of an async job.
Stops an async job by ID.

post.summary: Stop all jobs in a group
post.description: Stops all jobs in a stats group.
Executes multiple RC commands in one request.
Starts a file serving protocol (HTTP, WebDAV, FTP, SFTP, NFS, etc.).
Stops a running server by ID.
Stops all running servers.
Returns all running file servers.
Returns available protocol types.
Returns current global options or specific blocks.
Sets global rclone options at runtime.
Returns option types, defaults, and descriptions.
Returns names of all option blocks.
Returns local config and filter options (useful for checking overrides).
Expires cache entries for a path.
Ensures file chunks are cached locally.
Returns cache backend statistics.
Runs a backend-specific command.
Clears the internal filesystem object cache.
Returns number of items in fs cache.
Adds a web GUI plugin from GitHub.
Returns loaded and test plugins.
Returns plugins matching a MIME/plugin type.
Returns loaded test plugins.
Removes an installed plugin.
Removes a test plugin.
Sets Go block profile rate.
Sets Go GC target percentage.
Sets mutex contention profile fraction.
Sets Go runtime soft memory limit.
